key_insight: SAGEAgent treats multimodal survival prediction as a sequential decision problem, learning when to stop or continue collecting diagnostic data to balance accuracy with patient burden.
clinical_relevance: This approach could help oncology teams avoid unnecessary tests by supporting cost-aware, ordered modality acquisition while still aiming for reliable survival estimates.
method: LLM-based clinical agent decides the next modality to acquire for each patient.
Uses clinical tools to convert numeric predictions into text, plus episodic memory for similar cases and semantic memory for reusable decision patterns.
Optimizes over an ordered diagnostic workflow with escalating burden, from demographics to genomics.
evidence_signal: On a glioma cohort spanning TCGA-LGG, TCGA-GBM, and BraTS with four modalities, SAGEAgent matched competitive survival prediction performance while reducing average acquisition burden by 55%.
watch_item: The workflow is evaluated in a specific glioma setting, so broader validation is needed before use across other cancers, institutions, and real clinical workflows.
paper_title: SAGEAgent: A Self-Evolving Agent for Cost-Aware Modality Acquisition in Multimodal Survival Prediction
